SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper states clear claims and supports them with large-scale experiments (3.3M instances, 80/5/15 split) comparing GRU, LSTM, feed-forward and random-forest baselines and reports micro-AUC and label-ranking loss (Sections 3.1–4). The experimental design is generally sound (patient-level split, hyperparameter random search, reasonable baselines), but lacks statistical significance testing or confidence intervals to quantify differences between models. There are also pragmatic compromises (e.g., random-forest trained as an ensemble on data subsets, and feed-forward optimization choices) that could affect fairness of comparisons, and the authors acknowledge label-noise in medication data which complicates interpretation. Overall the results justify the main technical conclusions though minor methodological transparency (statistical tests, more baseline tuning details) is missing.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The work addresses a specific, well-motivated prediction problem—inferring active medication therapeutic classes from sequences of billing codes—which the authors argue has not been studied in the same way (Sections 1 and 2.3). Using recurrent models (GRU/LSTM) at this scale (3.3M instances) and explicitly comparing recurrence to a similarly-configured feed-forward net to isolate temporal benefits is a notable and non-trivial application-level contribution. That said, the methods (RNNs, embeddings, multi-label metrics) are established, and related prior work has applied GRUs to medical sequence prediction, so the novelty is primarily in problem formulation, scale, and empirical comparison rather than fundamentally new algorithms.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (background -> data -> models -> evaluation -> results) and defines key choices and metrics (e.g., micro-AUC, label-ranking loss) with equations (Section 3.4). Implementation and hyperparameter details are provided (Appendix A), and illustrative case analyses (Appendix C) help interpretation. Some minor presentation issues (occasional textual repetition/typos in the provided extract and references to figures not shown) do not obscure the main points; overall notation and exposition are clear and understandable.
